coredata.dc:title: Analysis of lead forms and transition in agricultural soil by nano-fluorescence method
coredata.dc:creator: Liu, Shuyu
Ni, Lv
Chen, Wenli
Wang, Jiangli
Ma, Fang
coredata.dc:description: The physicochemical properties of gold nanorods were used to develop many methods and techniques of detecting heavy metals recently. In this paper, the feasibility of gold nanorods was studied to detect metal lead in agricultural soil. The effects of soil properties on the form change of lead in soil were explored by gold nanorod detection technology. The results showed that the humic acid significantly increased Pb mobility and ion state exchanging. It also increased the lead content of organic bound state and Fe-Mn oxides state. And the detection process by gold nanorods proved to be a more simple and convenient method.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Heavy metal
Pb
Transition
Nano-fluorescence
Analysis
Agriculture soil

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Experimental method for detecting heavy metal lead in soil by gold nanorods
The linear equations of different forms of Pb in soil by nano-fluorescence analysis
Effect of humic acid and soil cation exchange capacity
Effect of humic acid on the lead contents detection process

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Agricultural soil sampling point in Shihezi University experimental field.
Sequential extraction heavy metal Pb in Soil.
Schematic representation of colorimetric method for Pb2+detection mechanism in S2O3 2−.
The linear relationship of the detection process of Pb.
Effect of humic acid on soil cation exchange capacity and the morphological transformation of lead.
-1 The detection process of Pb content affected by humic acid. -2 The detection process of Pb content affected by humic acid.
Determination of lead in soil (-----not detected).

head.ce:title: Analysis of lead forms and transition in agricultural soil by nano-fluorescence method

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Environment and Chemical Engineering

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: The physicochemical properties of gold nanorods were used to develop many methods and techniques of detecting heavy metals recently. In this paper, the feasibility of gold nanorods was studied to detect metal lead in agricultural soil. The effects of soil properties on the form change of lead in soil were explored by gold nanorod detection technology. The results showed that the humic acid significantly increased Pb mobility and ion state exchanging. It also increased the lead content of organic bound state and Fe-Mn oxides state. And the detection process by gold nanorods proved to be a more simple and convenient method.

ce:section.ce:para: Heavy metal pollution in agricultural soils was a serious environmental problem that affected the crops growth and the humans health through food chain accumulation. With the industrialization, urbanization and the abuse of chemical fertilizers and pesticides, the pollution of heavy metals was further aggravated. At present, the detection method of heavy metal ions had high accuracy, but there were also some obvious deficiencies, such as time consuming and expensive. Therefore, it was necessary to explore new method for detecting heavy metals in soil. Heavy metal lead mainly affected human health through food, drinking water, air, etc. It was toxic and extremely harmful to human body. Lead and its compounds will enter the body and be nerve, hematopoietic, digestive, kidney, cardiovascular and multiple systems such as endocrine caused harm (Liu and Li, 2019; Liu et al., 2017; Tang and Casas, 2014). Atomic Absorption Spectrometry for the determination of heavy metals as a traditional method, was also time consumed and laborious to operate and expensive. Therefore, this paper explored nanoscale detection methods of lead in soil.

With the development of nanotechnology, many application technologies have been developed based on gold nanorods, including the detection of biological macromolecules (Liu and Tan, 2018; Wang et al., 2017; Wang et al., 2016; Zhang et al., 2017;, 2015;), the releasing of controlled drugs (), photo-thermal therapy (Zhu et al., 2014; Soltan Monem et al., 2014) and heavy metals. Colorimetric detection and other aspects had developed rapidly. The method based on gold nanorods had good selectivity and sensitivity, and can determine the existence of heavy metals in a short time, which has important guiding significance for environmental protection and monitoring. By using the change of longitudinal surface plasmon resonance absorption (LSPR) of gold nanorods, which was convenient for detecting heavy metal lead in biological samples, and proved that the presence of lead accelerated the etching speed of gold nano-materials (Lan and Lin, 2014; Lee and Huang, 2011). The existence of copper was detected by synthesizing new compounds and gold nanorods and the variation of scattering intensity, which has high sensitivity and selectivity (Jing et al., 2014). Thereby the detection limit was very low. A linear relationship between the copper ion concentrations and the peaks change were set to judge the copper content (Zhang et al., 2014a). The photo-thermal effect of gold nanorods and relationship between copper ion concentration and temperature changes were not interfered by other ions (Xu et al., 2017a; Guan et al., 2015). The sensitivity and selectivity were good (Chen et al., 2017). In this study, the feasibility of gold nanorods was studied to detect heavy metal lead in agricultural soils. The effects of soil properties on the changes of lead forms in soil were explored by gold nanorod detection technology, which would show a more rapid and sensible analysis process to illustrate the heavy metal Pb migration and transition in agricultural soil.

Preparation of seed solution: 5 mL of CTAB (Cetyltrimethylammonium bromide) with concentration of 0.2 M was prepared, and mixed with 5 mL of concentration (0.5 mM) of chloroauric acid solution, and then 1 g of ice was added. Preparation of growth solution: 62.5 mL of a mixed solution. Synthesis and purification of gold nanorods: 0.1 ml seed solution was added to the growth solution, stirred and placed in a constant temperature water bath at 30 °C, reacted for 20 h, centrifuged at 7000 rpm for half an hour, washed with water to remove excess CTAB, and diluted with water (Nikoobakht and Sayed, 2003).

The soil was selected from agricultural soil in Shihezi city, Xinjiang district (East Trail 84′58″-86′24″, north latitude 43′26″-45′20″) (Fig. 1 ), with an average altitude of 450.8 m, 150 km east of Urumqi region. The particle size was between 0.02 mm and 0.2 mm. The soil cation exchange capacity was 21.81 cmol/kg and pH was 7.46.

The soil samples first removed the weeds, grass roots, stones, fertilizer pellets and other debris, then were air-dried under natural conditions, sieved (2 mm aperture sieve) and mixed (Qi et al., 2018). Morphological analysis was carried out by sequential extraction method. Heavy metals were obtained according to Tessier(1979) continuous fractionation method, including the exchange state, carbonate state, iron-manganese oxide bound state, organic bound state and residual state (Tessier et al., 1979). The flow chart of the leaching method was shown in Fig. 2 .

ce:section.ce:section-title: Experimental method for detecting heavy metal lead in soil by gold nanorods
ce:section.ce:para: It was a different mechanism of detecting heavy lead by Gold nanorods. It was by connecting the thiol group of glutathione with the surface of gold nanorods, and the excess amino carboxyl group was combined with Pb2+. The intensity of the two characteristic absorption peaks changed, and its change was related to the Pb concentration (Manjumeena et al., 2015; Xu et al., 2017b). Gold nanorods were tested by functioned gold nanorods with 20 μL of 0.1 M glutathione first, followed by addition of 80 μL of Pb solution at 25℃ for 50 min, then it was detected by UV–vis spectrophotometer (UV8453, Agilent, USA).

Soil properties would affect the morphology of heavy metals. The organic matter content, soil pH, and soil cation exchange capacity were all related to the transformation of heavy metals. The measurement principle of soil cation exchange amount was to use the mixture of 0.005 mol/LEDTA and 1 mol/L ammonium acetate as an exchange agent. The complexing agent can be exchanged with divalent calcium ions, magnesium ions, ferric ions, aluminum ions, etc., and the formation of ionization was minimal and stable (Yu et al., 2018; Gong et al., 2018a). At the same time, due to the presence of the ammonium acetate buffer, the exchangeable hydrogen and the monovalent metal ions can also be completely exchanged to form the saturated ammonium soil. The excess ammonium salt was washed away with 95% alcohol, and the exchange amount was determined by distillation. The effect of humid acid of 2 mg/kg (OM2), 50 mg/kg (OM50), 100 mg/kg (OM100) and blank control group (OM0) on lead in soil were studied. Total Contents of Lead and their Morphological Contents were as Table 1 .

The detection mechanism was shown in Fig. 3 . Sodium thiosulfate caused slight etch of gold nanorods, and the presence of lead accelerated the etching speed of gold nanorods, which resulted in two characteristic absorption peaks of gold nanorods. The degree of change was related to the lead concentration.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The linear equations of different forms of Pb in soil by nano-fluorescence analysis
ce:section.ce:para: The positively charged metal ions and the gold nanorods produced electrostatic shielding (Ferhan et al., 2010), that led to certain aggregation produyced by gold nanorods and its changing of characteristic absorption peaks (Zhang et al., 2014b). (R0-R)/R0 was used to illustrate the detection process. Among them, R0 represented the absorbance of the blank LSPR(Longitudinal surface plasmon resonance) to the TSPR(Transverse surfaceplasmon resonance) absorbance, and R represented absorbance of the gold nanorod LSPR of the experimental group to the TSPR absorbance. (R0-R)/R0 represented the degree of lead etching on gold nanorods (Lan and Lin, 2014). A series of lead concentrations were used as the solute, which determined the linear relationship between (R0-R)/R0 and the concentration of lead respectively. Since the residual state digestion solution cannot be configured, the residue after leaching of the undisturbed soil needed to be mixed and acid-dissolved as a residual solvent, followed by using nanometer fluorescence method to measure the lead.

As shown in Fig. 4 , the actual soil extract was tested for heavy metal Pb. The (R0-R)/R0 values of different heavy metal forms were determined by UV–vis spectrophotometer, and the heavy metals concentration were determined by linear relationship. The wavelength of TSPR was 550 nm and wavelength of LSR was 760 nm.

According to the lead standard solution of the leaching solution, heavy metal lead in soil using gold nanorods was determined. The linear relationship of the four forms lead was shown in Fig. 4. Since the ion exchange state of lead was not detected, its (R0-R)/R0 was lower than the (R0-R)/R0 of the 0 μM standard disposed in the soil extract. That the heavy metal lead was difficult to enter the soil crystal lattice, so the (R0-R)/R0 of the lead in the residual state was equal to or slightly lower than the 0 μM standard lead solution. As the residual state Pb should be changed to EXC-Pb to be detected that its detection equation and relationship was similar with EXC-Pb, as the R2 were both 0.96. The residual state digesting solution was similar to the mother liquid (R0-R)/R0.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Effect of humic acid and soil cation exchange capacity
ce:section.ce:para: The pH of the undisturbed soil was 7.46, and the pH of the blank group changed little after aging. The soil pH was significantly reduced by humic acid, and the more the humic acid existed, the more the pH reduced. This indicated that the hydrogen ion of humic acid was related to the content of humic acid, which led to the changing of Pb forms (Xu et al., 2016).

As shown in Fig. 5 , addition of humic acid significantly increased the amount of cation exchange in the soil, and the amount of cation exchange increased humic acid. That humic acid reduced the pH of the soil, which led to the increase of metal mobility and converting to ion exchange state (Xu et al., 2016; Lahori et al., 2017; Kautenburger et al., 2014). Another reason was that the exchangeable cations by humic acid itself increased the amount of cation exchange in the soil (Gong et al., 2018b).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Effect of humic acid on the lead contents detection process
ce:section.ce:para: The total amount of lead was shown in Fig. 6 . The recovery rate of humic acid of 2 mg/kg was 81.76%, the recovery rate of humic acid of 50 mg/kg was 129.33%, and the recovery rate of humic acid of 100 mg/kg was 101.55%. Humic acid significantly increased the lead content of ion exchange form and the carbonate-bound lead content was reduced. Because the rich active hydrogen in humic acid led to soil pH decreasing, made pH-sensitive carbonate of lead converting into ion exchange state, which improved the mobility of lead. Fe-Mn oxides also increased with the pH increasing. That the additional humic acid contained Fe-Mn oxides lead, and the soluble group dissolved by humic acid co-precipitates with lead on the surface of the soil formed Fe-Mn oxides. The large amount of functional groups contained in humic acid chelate and ion exchange lead formed organic matter bound (Xu et al., 2016; Qian et al., 2016). The content of organic bound state of lead changed little and the aging time was short. As the heavy metal form of lead is not stable, it was inclined to form the organic binding states. The residual state of lead is measured in the undisturbed soil.

It can be seen that the effect of humic acid on the morphological transformation of heavy metals lead embodies two functions of humic acid. Humic acid reduced soil pH and carbonate-bound lead content. The content of ion exchange state increased, but the content of organic bound state did not change much. The surface functional group of humic acid combined with lead to increase the content of organic bound state lead and some soluble groups released by humic acid coprecipitating with Pb increased Fe-Mn oxides lead.

An relationship equation between the variation of characteristic absorption intensity and the concentration of Pb was established, which can quickly detect the Pb transforming process. The humic acid in the soil significantly increased the amount of cation exchange, metal mobility and converting to ion exchange state. The surface functional group of humic acid combined with Pb increased the content of organic bound state Pb and increased Fe-Mn oxides Pb by humic acid. Which also affected the detection process of the different form of Pb.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This research was Supported by Open Project of State Key Laboratory of Urban Water Resource and Environment, Harbin Institute of Technology (No. ES201910). And Jiading District Agriculture and Social Undertaking Scientific Project (201914).
